response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with genuine distributed-systems exposure (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby) and long-standing craftsmanship practices, but he falls well short of the role's core minimum qualifications. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; his C++ work amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project. His recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product engineering rather than cloud compute platform or infrastructure work, and he has no formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers, which is central to this position.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Broad full-stack and polyglot background with strong testing/TDD culture and mentoring via workshops and courses
Long-running open-source ownership (GISCollective, D test tooling) showing ability to drive projects end-to-end
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ experience (under 1 year professionally)
No dedicated cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership; recent work is product-focused web development
No formal technical-lead role leading cross-team strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-performance backend exposure
